access: the ability to get in or use something
accommodation: a place to stay, or adjusting to fit
accountability: being answerable for what you do
adjustment: a change made to fit a new situation
administration: the people running a place or system
agenda: a list of what someone plans to do, sometimes hidden
agent: someone who acts on behalf of another
aggregate: a total collected from many parts
ambiguity: being unclear or having more than one meaning
animal: a living creature that isn't a plant
apparatus: a system or device set up to do a job
application: using something for a purpose, or a formal request
aspiration: something you hope and aim for
attitude: your outlook or stance toward something
authority: the power to tell others what to do
balance: equal weight on both sides, or stability
benefit: something good that comes from something
body: a physical living form, or a group
budget: a plan for how to spend money
burden: something heavy to carry or deal with
capacity: how much something can hold or do
capital: money used to make more money, or a city of power
career: the work you do through your life
casualty: a person killed or wounded, usually in war
challenge: something difficult, or a polite word for a problem
channel: a path through which something flows
civilian: a person not in the military
client: a customer, usually of a professional service
closure: an ending, or the feeling that something is finished
comfort: ease and freedom from pain or worry
community: a group of people living or working together
compensation: payment for work or for harm done
competency: the ability to do something adequately
compliance: doing what you are told to do
condition: the state something is in, or a requirement
consequence: what happens as a result of something
content: what something contains, or feeling satisfied
cooperation: working together toward a shared goal
cost: what you pay or give up
culture: the shared beliefs and practices of a group
deficit: having less than you need, especially money
demand: a firm request, or the need for something
deployment: sending out people or equipment for a purpose
development: growth or improvement, often slow and planned
difficulty: something hard to do or deal with
disability: a condition limiting what someone can do
disadvantage: something putting you at a worse position
diversity: a variety of different types of people
domestic: relating to the home or one's own country
economy: the system of money, goods, and work in a society
emergency: a sudden dangerous situation needing fast action
employee: a person paid to work for someone
enhance: to make something better or more intense
escalation: making something bigger, worse, or more intense
executive: a high-ranking person in a company
expectation: something you believe will happen or is required
facility: a place built for a specific purpose
fatality: a death, especially in an accident or conflict
federal: relating to the national central government
feedback: response telling how well something worked
force: physical power, or a body of armed people
foundation: the base on which something is built
freedom: the state of not being controlled or imprisoned
government: the group that rules a country
health: the condition of a person's body or mind
humanitarian: concerned with helping people in need
incentive: something that motivates you to do something
incident: an event, usually bad or unusual
inclusion: making sure everyone is part of something
industry: businesses that make or do the same kind of thing
infrastructure: the basic systems a society needs to function
integration: bringing parts together as a whole
intelligence: the ability to learn and understand, or secret information
interaction: acting on or with each other
interest: wanting to know more, or money paid on a loan
intervention: getting involved to change a situation
investment: spending money to make more money
issue: a problem or a point being discussed
legacy: what you leave behind after you're gone
leverage: using something to gain an advantage
liberal: open to change and new ideas, or relating to certain politics
limitation: something that restricts what is possible
management: the people in charge of something, or the act of controlling
market: a place or system for buying and selling
mechanism: a system or process that produces a result
media: means of mass communication like TV, radio, internet
model: a simplified version used to understand something
momentum: the force of movement, or gathering energy toward a goal
narrative: a story or account, often shaped to persuade
network: a system of connected things or people
neutralize: to make something harmless or ineffective
objective: a goal you are trying to achieve
obligation: something you are required to do
operation: a series of actions toward a goal, especially military or medical
opportunity: a chance to do or get something
organization: a group of people with a shared purpose
oversight: watching over and checking something, or accidentally missing it
paradigm: a standard way of thinking about something
perception: how something appears or is understood
performance: how well something or someone does a job
personnel: the people employed by an organization
philosophy: the study of fundamental questions about life and reality
policy: a rule or principle guiding decisions
population: all the people in a place
position: where something is, or a job, or a stance
practice: doing something regularly, or how something is usually done
priority: something more important than other things
privilege: a special advantage not everyone has
procedure: a set of steps for doing something
productivity: how much useful work gets done
profession: a paid job requiring special training
professional: someone trained and paid in a skilled field
protocol: an official set of rules or steps
provision: something provided, or a clause in an agreement
relationship: a connection between people or things
resource: something useful that can be drawn on
responsibility: having a duty to do something or for something
restructuring: reorganizing how something is set up, often with job cuts
rights: things you are morally or legally entitled to
risk: the chance of something bad happening
role: the function someone or something plays
security: being safe from harm or protected
service: work done for others, or the military
settlement: an agreement reached, or a place people have built homes
social: relating to society and people together
stability: being steady and not likely to change suddenly
stakeholder: someone with an interest in the outcome
status: position in a group or society
strategy: a plan for achieving a long-term goal
structure: the arrangement of parts forming something
sustainability: being able to continue without using things up
system: a set of connected parts that work together
technology: tools and methods developed from scientific knowledge
territory: an area of land under someone's control
tolerance: accepting something you might not agree with
tradition: a practice handed down through generations
transition: moving from one state or situation to another
transparency: being open and not hiding things
treatment: the way someone is dealt with, or medical care
trust: believing someone is reliable and honest
value: how much something is worth, or a principle you hold
violence: using physical force to hurt or kill
vision: the ability to see, or a long-term goal
welfare: health and happiness, or government financial support
workforce: all the people employed in a place
